Locate the RandAugment function or class definition where the magnitude standard deviation is computed (around line 1566 in augment.py)
Prepare to generate test input parameters for RandAugment, specifically setting the magnitude level and level_std parameters explicitly (e.g., level=5, level_std=0.5)
Create a dummy input image tensor with fixed size, e.g., shape (1, 224, 224, 3), filled with arbitrary values (e.g., tf.ones or random uniform between 0 and 1)
Call the RandAugment augmentation function/module with the dummy input image and the magnitude parameters set as above
Within the test, patch or instrument the code to capture the internally computed magnitude after the line with `+=` that should include level_std multiplication
Run the augmentation multiple times (e.g., 100 iterations) with the same level and level_std to collect the applied magnitudes
Check the variance (standard deviation) of the collected magnitude values across iterations
Assert that the standard deviation of the magnitude values is either 0 or 1, indicating the bug that level_std is not being applied as a multiplier and is fixed at 1
